seed_and_followups.seed: I recently received some positive feedback from my manager regarding my performance on a recent task.
followups: The feedback came a week after I submitted my report, which was a relief since I put a lot of effort into it.
Knowing that my hard work was recognized made me feel appreciated, even if it was just a modest acknowledgment.
I find it uplifting to receive any level of praise; it motivates me to keep pushing myself at work.
I’m considering asking for more regular feedback to help me continue improving, but I’m also curious about how I can exceed their expectations.

seed_and_followups.seed: I just got some feedback from my manager about my last project, and it was pretty positive, which feels nice.
followups: It turns out I did some things right, but I was a bit surprised to hear it because I thought I could have done better.
Receiving that feedback definitely lifted my spirits; it's reassuring to know my efforts are recognized, even if it's just a little.
I mean, it's not a huge win, but it does make me feel like I'm on the right track, and that motivates me to keep improving.
I think I want to build on this momentum; maybe I'll ask for more feedback on my next project to keep this positive vibe going.
